candidates.why: Exact match for total current assets on the balance sheet.
Balancing line, not the reported total current assets.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit reported total current assets line.

candidates.why: Exact IFRS-style cash balance sheet label; preferred over alternatives like cash in hand and at bank.

candidates.row_label: Cash in hand and at bank
candidates.why: Could represent cash, but a cleaner exact cash-and-cash-equivalents label exists.

final_choice.row_label: Cash in hand and at bank

variables.notes: Priority list provided because labels vary by year; 'Cash and cash equivalents' is preferred, with 'Cash in hand and at bank' as fallback.

candidates.why: Exact match for total current liabilities.
Component only, not total current liabilities.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit reported total current liabilities line.

candidates.why: Explicit current maturities of long-term debt; directly relevant to short-term debt.
Short-term financing instrument and relevant for current debt when reported separately.
Appears under current-liability debt breakdown in some years, suggesting current portion of bonds.
Interest-bearing current debt component when separately disclosed; excludes leases by label.

variables.notes: No single unambiguous current-debt total is uniquely identifiable because 'Financial liabilities' is duplicated for current and non-current sections. Chose a debt-component set excluding leases, but review is warranted to confirm no overlap across components in any year.

candidates.why: Best match for taxes payable/current tax liabilities on the balance sheet.
Tax asset, opposite of taxes payable.

variables.notes: Chosen outside shortlist; confident this is the current tax payable/liability line.

candidates.why: Direct reported PPE line; preferred over gross/accumulated component lines.
Could be gross tangible assets, but not populated and less preferable than direct PPE.

variables.notes: Selected the direct PPE line rather than gross/accumulated components.
Selected the explicit total assets line.

candidates.why: Despite unusual wording, values behave like operating cash flow: capital expenditure is shown below and free cash flow is calculated as this line minus capex.

candidates.row_label: FREE CASH FLOW (Cash flow from operations - Capital expenditure)
candidates.why: Not operating cash flow itself; explicitly after capex.

variables.notes: Chosen outside shortlist; confident this line represents cash flow from operations based on statement structure and the free cash flow reconciliation.
